channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: A Senate vote to reverse Trump's tariffs on Canada tests Republican support
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — With President Donald Trump's so-called “Liberation Day” of tariff implementation fast approaching, Senate Democrats are putting Republican support for some of those plans to the test by forcing a vote to nullify the emergency declaration that underpins the tariffs on Canada.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Booker talks through the night in marathon floor speech to protest Trump
item.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) pulled an all-nighter, delivering a marathon speech on the Senate floor that lasted well into Tuesday morning in a push to combat the Trump administration’s policies. Booker, a member of Democratic leadership, rose to speak around 7 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin voters to choose top education official and consider voter ID amendment
item.description: Wisconsin is electing its top education official, who will guide policies affecting K-12 schools during President Donald Trump’s second term. Tuesday’s vote pits the teachers union-backed incumbent Jill Underly against Republican-supported critic Brittany Kinser.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Election Day: Suburban voters head to the polls
item.description: Several elections will take place in Chicago-area suburbs on Tuesday, including a notable one in Dolton where incumbent Tiffany Henyard will run against Jason House, who won the primary election in February.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Dortch, WGN 9

item.title: Mass layoffs are underway at the nation's public health agencies
item.description: Employees across the massive U.S. Health and Human Services Department received notices Tuesday that their jobs were being eliminated, part of a sweeping overhaul designed to vastly shrink the agencies responsible for protecting and promoting Americans’ health.
item.dc:creator: Carla K. Johnson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Inside a Coast Guard border crackdown
item.description: Chief Peter Nelson, officer in charge for Coast Guard Station San Diego, described escalating confrontations with smugglers.
item.dc:creator: Brian Entin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's pick for Joint Chiefs chairman vows to be apolitical and addresses Signal chat
item.description: President Donald Trump’s nominee to become the next chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff acknowledges he's an unconventional nominee.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Minnesota union employees demand Walz halt half-time in office policy
item.description: Around 500 Minnesota state employees protested Saturday, calling on DFL Gov. Tim Walz to reverse course on a policy to require more in-office work. The governor says having more employees spend at least half their time at their workplaces will improve communication and collaboration.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Odds of a recession are rising, top economists warn
item.description: Goldman Sachs now sees a 35% chance of a U.S. recession in the next 12 months, up from 20% previously.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Wisconsin Supreme Court race draws national attention, record out-of-state money
item.description: Zac Schultz, a reporter with PBS Wisconsin’s “Here & Now” program, has been closely covering the race and joined MPR News host Cathy Wurzer on Morning Edition with the highlights.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Other states should work on banning food dyes: WV state senator
item.description: This ban will start for meals served in school nutrition programs starting this August and go statewide starting on Jan. 1, 2028.
item.dc:creator: Marni Hughes, NewsNation

item.title: Suspected Tren de Aragua, MS-13 members sent to El Salvador
item.description: Suspected Venezuelan gang members and criminals were among the 17 migrants sent to El Salvador by the U.S. military.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Trump faces crucial week on the economy
item.description: President Trump is entering a critical week for the economy amid growing fears that his penchant for tariffs could stall growth and undercut progress on inflation.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Catholic Charities tests Wisconsin’s unemployment payment system at Supreme Court
item.description: A chapter of Catholic Charities in Wisconsin contends it should be exempted from the state’s unemployment compensation system because it’s a charitable organization with a religious mission.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: FCC chair opens investigation into Disney and ABC over DEI practices
item.description: FCC chair Brendan Carr said he wants a review all of Disney's DEI practices — both past and present — to determine whether the company has ever violated any FCC regulations.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: Trump says he’s ‘very angry’ and ‘pissed off’ at Putin during NBC News interview
item.description: President Donald Trump said he was “very angry” and “pissed off” when Russian President Vladimir Putin criticized the credibility of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s leadership, adding that the comments were “not going in the right location.
item.dc:creator: Kristin Welker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Richard Carlbom ascends to top role at Minnesota DFL Party as its first new chair in 14 years
item.description: Richard Carlbom was chosen as the Minnesota DFL Party chair, rising to the top post after years of working behind the scenes on party elections and causes.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News
